zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William de Graham Lord of Abercorn and Dalkeith於大約1097年出生在英國林可爾史爾格朗坦時，他的父親Alan de Graham18歲，母親Lucia Mercia Ramera28歲。他和Lillian Doran至少有5個兒子。他去世於1139年之後在英國蘇格蘭米德洛迪安達爾凱斯。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Graham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Grantham in Lincolnshire, recorded in Domesday Book as Graham (as well as Grantham, Grandham, and Granham). See also Grantham .

Irish (especially Ulster): in Ireland Graham is generally the Scottish name, but it has additionally been adopted as an Anglicized form of both Ó Creacháin (see Crehan ) and Ó Gormghaile (see Gormley ).

History: The surname Graham was taken to Scotland at the beginning of the 12th century by the Norman baron William de Graham, holder of the manor of Grantham in Lincolnshire, from whom many if not all modern bearers are probably descended. The chief of Clan Graham is the Duke of Montrose.

variants.name: Greim
meanings: South German: from a short form of a compound personal name beginning with the element grīm ‘mask, helmet’.

variants.name: Grahm
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire and northern England): shortened form of   Graham  .

variants.name: Grantham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire and East Yorkshire): habitational name from Grantham in Lincolnshire, of uncertain origin. The final element is Old English hām ‘homestead’; the first may be Old English grand ‘gravel’ or perhaps a personal name Granta, which probably originated as a byname meaning ‘snarler’. See also Graham .

variants.name: Grahame
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Graham  . — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is as high as 261, while the figure for 2010 is supposedly lower than 100.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William de Graham Lord of Abercorn and Dalkeith was born about 1097, in Grantham, Lincolnshire, England, his father, Alan de Graham, was 18 and his mother, Lucia Mercia Ramera, was 28. He had at least 5 sons with Lillian Doran. He died after 1139, in Dalkeith, Midlothian, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William de Graham Lord of Abercorn and Dalkeith est né environ 1097, à Grantham, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, Alan de Graham, avait 18 ans et sa mère, Lucia Mercia Ramera, avait 28 ans. Il a eu au moins 5 fils avec Lillian Doran. Il est décédé après 1139, à Dalkeith, Midlothian, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William de Graham Lord of Abercorn and Dalkeith um 1097 in Grantham, Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Alan de Graham 18 und seine Mutter Lucia Mercia Ramera 28. Er hatte mit Lillian Doran mindestens 5 Söhne. Er starb nach 1139 in Dalkeith, Midlothian, Schottland, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William de Graham Lord of Abercorn and Dalkeith è nato il circa 1097 nel Grantham, Lincolnshire, England, il suo padre, Alan de Graham, aveva 18 anni e il suo madre, Lucia Mercia Ramera, aveva 28 anni. Lui avuto almeno 5 figli con Lillian Doran. Morì dopo il 1139, nel Dalkeith, Midlothian, Scozia, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William de Graham Lord of Abercorn and Dalkeithが約1097年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアグランサムで生まれた時，父Alan de Grahamは18歳，母Lucia Mercia Rameraは28歳であった。彼はLillian Doranとの間に少なくとも5人の息子をもうけた。彼は1139年以後にイギリススコットランドミドロスアンダルケイスで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William de Graham Lord of Abercorn and Dalkeith(이)가 대략 1097년 영국 링코실르 그랜섬에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Alan de Graham(은)는 18세였고, 그의 어머니 Lucia Mercia Ramera(은)는 28세였다. 그는 Lillian Doran(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1139년 이후 영국 스코틀랜드 미들로스이언 다케이스에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William de Graham Lord of Abercorn and Dalkeith nasceu aproximadamente 1097, em Grantham, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Alan de Graham, tinha 18 anos e sua mãe, Lucia Mercia Ramera, tinha 28 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 5 filhos com Lillian Doran. Ele faleceu após 1139, em Dalkeith, Midlothian, Escócia, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William de Graham Lord of Abercorn and Dalkeith родился около 1097 г. в местности грантам, линколншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Alan de Graham было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Lucia Mercia Ramera, 28. У него было по крайней мере 5 сыновей от Lillian Doran. Он умер после 1139 г. в местности далкит, мидлотян, Шотландия, Великобритания.

Tuvo por lo menos 5 hijos con Lillian Doran. Murió después de 1139, en Dalkeith, Midlothian, Escocia, Reino Unido.
